figures.caption_line: Fig. 9. Effects of (a, b, c) AI and Cr,(d, e, f) Ti and Nb,(g, h, i) Ta and Nb,(j, k, l) W and Mo on the ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ volume fraction (a, d, g, j), size (b, e, h, k) and roundness (c, f, i, l) in the Co-Ni-Al-W-Ti-Ta-Cr-Mo-Nb system predicted by machine learning models.

figures.caption_line: Fig. 10. (a) Scatter diagram (Volume fraction vs. size) for the alloys with spherical ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ precipitates (Roundness>0.77) and (b) distribution diagram for the total content of ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ formers (Al+Ti+Ta+Nb+0.5W), $\gamma$ ’ strengthening elements (Ti+Ta+Nb) and solid solution strengthening elements (W+Mo+Cr) of the alloys with cuboidal ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ precipitates $\left( {{0.70} \times  \text{ Roundness } \vartriangleleft  {0.72}}\right)$ after screening with various criteria in the Co-Ni-Al-W-Ti-Ta-Cr-Mo-Nb system predicted by machine learning models. Two selected alloys for validations are also shown (stars symbols).

(a) Scatter diagram (Volume fraction vs. size) for the alloys with spherical ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ precipitates (Roundness>0.77) and (b) distribution diagram for the total content of ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ formers (Al+Ti+Ta+Nb+0.5W), $\gamma$ ’ strengthening elements (Ti+Ta+Nb) and solid solution strengthening elements (W+Mo+Cr) of the alloys with cuboidal ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ precipitates $\left( {{0.70} \times  \text{ Roundness } \vartriangleleft  {0.72}}\right)$ after screening with various criteria in the Co-Ni-Al-W-Ti-Ta-Cr-Mo-Nb system predicted by machine learning models. Two selected alloys for validations are also shown (stars symbols).

figures.caption_line: Fig. 11. Typical microstructures of the alloys (a, b) A1 and (c, d) A2 after annealing at ${850}^{ \circ  }\mathrm{C}$ for ${1000}\mathrm{\;h}$ . No secondary phases were precipitated along the grain boundaries, indicating decent microstructural stability of the alloys A1 and A2.
figures.full_block:
![bo_d7fe34s91nqc73er9kc0_9_937_1048_671_458_0.jpg](images/bo_d7fe34s91nqc73er9kc0_9_937_1048_671_458_0.jpg)

Fig. 11. Typical microstructures of the alloys (a, b) A1 and (c, d) A2 after annealing at ${850}^{ \circ  }\mathrm{C}$ for ${1000}\mathrm{\;h}$ . No secondary phases were precipitated along the grain boundaries, indicating decent microstructural stability of the alloys A1 and A2.

figures.caption_line: Fig. 12. (a) APT reconstruction with a 14 at $\%$ cr isocomposition surface highlighted to illustrate the ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ precipitates and (b, c, d, e) composition profile across the $\gamma  \mid  {\gamma }^{\prime }$ interface derived using the proxigram approach. (d) Partitioning coefficients between the ${\gamma }^{\prime }$ and $\gamma$ phases $\left( {\mathrm{K}}_{{\gamma }^{\prime }/\gamma }\right)$ of alloy A1 after annealing at ${850}^{ \circ  }\mathrm{C}$ for 1000 h.
